title: Rei underreited
wikitext:
[]: Rei = underreited             Quote Post by Born of Lilith » Thu Aug 25, 2011 3:25 pm Yes, yes, of course, I know that Rei has a crapton of really devoted fans, some less creepy than others, to the point of *sigh* memetic mutation (ZOMG TEH Rei!1) but I don't seem to see a lot of fans who find her arresting, sort of mystifying, as a character rather than just the object of crazy otaku fetishes. I was actually initially tipped off to Eva from reading her Wikipedia article and being drawn to her character: mysterious and shrouded in anguish yet with obvious depth lurking beneath the surface, and I ended up incredibly frustrated with how she was sort of forced to regress back into her cold shell instead of fully realizing her own humanity in the series. I still find her to be the most interesting and perplexing character of the franchise. It really gave me some consolation to read an article written by her seiyuu that totally affirmed some of my pensive musings on Rei's misty, elusive character, but I just wanted to know if any of my fellow fanboys have similar thoughts and partialities towards her.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Aug 25, 2011 3:32 pm >suggesting Rei is not the most iconic and influential female anime character of the 90s which is not something one can explain through superficial fetishism from the audience, especially since she has so many girl fans oh, you...

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Aug 25, 2011 4:08 pm Xard wrote: >suggesting Rei is not the most iconic and influential female anime character of the 90s which is not something one can explain through superficial fetishism from the audience, especially since she has so many girl fans

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Aug 25, 2011 4:18 pm CJD wrote: I cannot put into words how much I simultaneously love and hate you for doing that.

[]:             Quote Post by Born of Lilith » Fri Aug 26, 2011 1:23 pm Another attempt at an actual discussion reduced to a circle jerk of snarky one-liners... and I wondered why I stopped posting here for a while. Xard wrote: >suggesting Rei is not the most iconic and influential female anime character of the 90s which is not something one can explain through superficial fetishism from the audience, especially since she has so many girl fans oh, you...

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 26, 2011 3:53 pm Personally I never notice much of this "underreiting" that Born of Lilith has mentioned, but maybe I've simply disregarded it. Anyway, I was deeply disappointed by Rei's regression in the original series as well, it was something that I never accepted and I've always hoped that it would be fixed sooner or later; now we have Rebuild and I'm quite satisfied with it, let's just hope that she won't regress once more...

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Fri Aug 26, 2011 3:59 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Personally I never notice much of this "underreiting" that Born of Lilith has mentioned, but maybe I've simply disregarded it. Anyway, I was deeply disappointed by Rei's regression in the original series as well, it was something that I never accepted and I've always hoped that it would be fixed sooner or later; now we have Rebuild and I'm quite satisfied with it, let's just hope that she won't regress once more...

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Fri Aug 26, 2011 6:19 pm I enjoyed her character arc in the original series leading into EoE. It felt right for her. Her devotion to Shinji coupled with her own conflict about her existence and being replaced drove her actions to a point that makes a lot of sense. Her ride was an interesting one to watch, and I've always felt satisfied with it. I'm still withholding my judgment on Rebuild!Rei until the series is complete...

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 26, 2011 7:18 pm Born of Lilith wrote: Another attempt at an actual discussion reduced to a circle jerk of snarky one-liners...

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri Aug 26, 2011 9:21 pm What's this character regression everyone keeps talking about?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 26, 2011 9:23 pm child of Lilith wrote: What's this character regression everyone keeps talking about?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 26, 2011 9:57 pm Bagheera wrote: Utter nonsense spouted by people who weren't paying attention.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 26, 2011 10:15 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Oh, this is really a good way to dismiss someone's opinion; we weren't paying attention while you're the only one who carefully watched Evangelion dozens of times, sure thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 26, 2011 10:20 pm Bagheera wrote: If you're claiming something that's obviously not true there's no other conclusion to be drawn.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 26, 2011 10:29 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: I suppose that you're referring about the first point, anyway it doesn't say anything about the bullshitness of my second point, the more important one.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 26, 2011 11:04 pm Bagheera wrote: That's completely invalidated by Rei's actions in EoE. Her memories may be fuzzy but the important shit is still there.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Fri Aug 26, 2011 11:06 pm Bagheera wrote: That's completely invalidated by Rei's actions in EoE. Her memories may be fuzzy but the important shit is still there.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 26, 2011 11:11 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Just the presence of the "important shit" doesn't exclude that there was a regression; there's no sense of continuity between Rei II and Rei III, she has feelings that are not felt as her own, she doesn't know what she's supposed to do with them, her only genuine desire is her own death (since she's almost returned to square one as far as her self-identification as human being is concerned).

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Aug 26, 2011 11:34 pm Bagheera wrote: And that lasts for about a minute (no not literally, it's just an expression) before Instrumentality hits and she gets every last bit of it back (and what was lost before she was Rei II besides) and then goes quantum and lives forever. There's no meaningful regression in her character.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Aug 26, 2011 11:36 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: There's at least a temporary regression; actually I'm not even sure if I can refer to her as a "character" after Instrumentality, she went beyond human existence to become some kind of God...
